title: API
description: Orient around the you-agent-factory OpenAPI and HTTP surface: session-scoped routes, preview, and representative calls against a running local factory host.
openingSummary: The you-agent-factory API is the HTTP contract a running factory host exposes. OpenAPI (Open Application Programming Interface) describes that contract so web readers can discover session-scoped routes without reverse-engineering CLI wrappers.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This reference covers the you-agent-factory API and OpenAPI surface for web readers: what the HTTP contract is for, the host and session nouns callers address, and how to scan the primary route families a live factory exposes. Use it when you need the published HTTP/OpenAPI orientation rather than a CLI matrix, MCP host setup, or a packaged docs sync.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: OpenAPI (Open Application Programming Interface) is the product contract that describes the HTTP API a running factory host exposes. Callers target that host—not this documentation site—and address work through session-scoped routes keyed by a session id. The primary address model is session-scoped: discover and inspect factory sessions, read status, submit work, and preview factories against the live host. Request bodies use OpenAPI camelCase field names such as workTypeName.

openapiSurface.title: OpenAPI Surface
openapiSurface.body: Scan the primary HTTP families a running factory host exposes. Paths are session-scoped unless noted; replace {session_id} with a live session id from discovery (often ~default on single-session local hosts).

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Start or attach a local factory so an HTTP host is listening, then call the session-scoped routes against that host. Use a live session id from session discovery—often ~default on single-session local hosts.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web OpenAPI and HTTP orientation for the you-agent-factory API surface. It is not a build-time generated OpenAPI UI, not a full schema dump, not a sync of packaged CLI docs, and not the Factory Session, submitting-work, dynamic-workflows, MCP, or CLI deep pages.

additionalRoutes.body: The same host also exposes additional session-scoped reads and controls—events (GET /factory-sessions/{session_id}/events), factory query (GET /factory-sessions/{session_id}/factory), pause and resume (POST /factory-sessions/{session_id}/pause and /resume), and staged-files upload (POST /factory-sessions/{session_id}/work/staged-files)—without changing the session-scoped address model.

links.endpointSessionsListPurpose: List live factory sessions on the host.
links.endpointSessionsShowPurpose: Read one live Factory Session projection.
links.endpointSessionStatusPurpose: Read lifecycle phase, runtime activity, and related status fields for one session.
links.endpointWorkSubmitPurpose: Submit one work item (required camelCase fields include name and workTypeName).
links.endpointPreviewPurpose: Validate source and policy without creating a session.
